title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019 across the globe, utilizing a continuous color scale ranging from dark red for a score of -4 to dark blue for a score of 4. The visualization indicates that large positive scores correspond to rare abuses, while large negative scores signify that abuses are relatively widespread, with the underlying data derived from a statistical model combining measures from several other sources. The highest values, falling in the dark blue band between 3 and 4, appear across western and central Europe including Norway, Sweden, and Germany, as well as in Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. A slightly lighter blue band, representing scores between 1 and 3, covers the United States, the United Kingdom, France, and much of South America including Argentina and Chile. In contrast, the lowest scores are visible in the Middle East and parts of Asia; Yemen, Syria, and Afghanistan sit in the darkest red band between -4 and -3, indicating widespread abuses. Surrounding nations such as Iraq, Iran, and Libya fall into the -2 to -1 range, colored in medium orange. Large portions of Africa display a mix of orange and light red shades, placing countries like Sudan, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo in the negative range between -1 and -3. Russia and China appear in the light orange band, corresponding to scores between -1 and 0. Greenland and several countries in central Africa are marked with diagonal hatching to denote no data. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of high protection scores in the Americas, Oceania, and Western Europe, while the lowest scores cluster in conflict-affected regions of the Middle East and specific areas of Africa and Asia.
